officialReports.name: COVID Counter-Fraud Commissioner Final Report (Hayhoe Review)
officialReports.author: Tom Hayhoe (independent commissioner appointed by Chancellor Rachel Reeves)
officialReports.headlineFindings: £10.91bn lost to COVID-19 fraud and error across all schemes; £1.79bn recovered to date; £762m of substandard PPE unlikely to ever be recovered. Litigation remains active or in prospect for 8 contracts. DHSC continues efforts to recover £122m settlement against PPE Medpro. Recommendations are forward-looking: treat fraud as core strategic risk in public programme design, use data more systematically, expect private-sector participants to carry greater compliance burden.
officialReports.scope: Review of COVID-era procurement and fraud across PPE, Bounce Back Loans, grants and other emergency spending under the previous government.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Baroness Mone (Conservative Peer) and husband Doug Barrowman
contracts.outcome: Court ordered £122m repayment Oct 2025. Gowns failed sterilisation. Company wound up Dec 2025 with £148m owed to government. Only ~£600k available for unsecured creditors. HMRC separately owed ~£39m. Recovery of taxpayer funds considered unlikely.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Owen Paterson MP was paid consultant (8,333/month since 2015). Lobbied Matt Hancock via texts.
contracts.outcome: Paterson resigned after lobbying scandal. Failed to trace 30% of contacts. No penalty clauses.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.product: PPE (gowns, eye protectors, masks) and freight logistics
contracts.connection: Referred by Lord Agnew (Cabinet Office Minister). Shared address with Julia Lopez MP constituency.
contracts.outcome: 182.8m items unusable. 178.5m wasted. Company profits jumped 500%.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Steve Dechan, former Conservative councillor and party donor. 10-employee company with 486k loss in 2019.
contracts.outcome: Company had no PPE track record. Described as 'reeking of cronyism' by campaigners.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Brooks Newmark (former Conservative MP) acted as adviser. Referred by Lord Agnew. Broker Zoe Ley made 17.6m.
contracts.outcome: 106m of PPE unfit for purpose.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Adviser Andrew Mills was also government Board of Trade adviser. Connection to Liz Truss.
contracts.outcome: All 50m masks unsuitable for NHS (wrong strap type). 150m+ wasted. Court ruled VIP lane unlawful.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Miami-based fashion/jewellery entrepreneur with no PPE experience. VIP lane referral.
contracts.outcome: Priced 50%+ above market rate. Multiple contracts challenged by Good Law Project.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.product: Hand sanitiser and face masks (6 contracts)
contracts.connection: David Meller: Conservative donor (60k+ since 2009), trustee Policy Exchange, Michael Gove leadership campaign finance chair.
contracts.outcome: 8.46m unsuitable for NHS. Cost 12.64/unit vs 5.87 average. Company admitted deleting emails Feb 2024.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: VIP lane referral. Co Antrim sweet wholesaler (coffee, nuts, confectionery) with no PPE experience.
contracts.outcome: PPE sold at 99.5% loss (1,000 box sold for 5). Judicial review by Good Law Project.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Dormant company with 100 in assets, no website, registered at accountant's address.
contracts.outcome: No prior track record in PPE or medical supplies.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Director met DHSC chief commercial officer at a birthday party. Pest control company.
contracts.outcome: Contract value disputed (32m to 347m). Government claimed 108m figure issued 'in error'.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.product: Management consulting for NHS Test and Trace
contracts.connection: Dido Harding (Baroness Harding, Conservative Peer) appointed to run Test & Trace. Married to Conservative MP John Penrose.
contracts.outcome: 1m per day on Deloitte consultants. Senior rates up to 6,000/day. PAC found no clear evidence scheme reduced infections.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Awarded without competition. Serco previously fined 100m for prison tagging fraud.
contracts.outcome: Failed to trace 30% of contacts. Data breach (300 tracers details shared). No penalty clauses.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.connection: Non-competitive award. Senior consultants billed at 7,000/day.
contracts.outcome: Daily rates equated to 1.5m annual salary equivalent per consultant.

contracts.department: NHS / Cabinet Office
contracts.connection: David Cameron (former PM) joined as adviser at 720k+ salary. Lex Greensill held Downing Street business card as Crown Representative since 2014.
contracts.outcome: Cameron earned 8m total. Greensill collapsed 2021. Cameron lobbied Sunak for COVID financing (refused). Not found to have broken rules but called for rule strengthening.

contracts.department: Cabinet Office
contracts.connection: 6 contracts of 30m each awarded to McKinsey, Bain, KPMG, Accenture, Deloitte, PwC without competition.
contracts.outcome: Potential extension to 360m total. Described as 'outsourcing Brexit'.

contracts.department: Dept of Health / MOJ / DfE
contracts.connection: 420 contracts with UK public sector. Directors awarded CBEs. 45% of revenue from public sector.
contracts.outcome: Collapsed Jan 2018. Royal Liverpool Hospital 125m over budget. 220 schools affected. 30 councils disrupted. Government abolished PFI Oct 2018.

contracts.department: Dept of Health
contracts.connection: New Labour policy of using PFI for all major hospital builds. Total repayment obligation 82bn for 13bn capital.
contracts.outcome: Annual NHS cost 890m by 2009. Hospitals 25% fewer beds. 333 to change a lightbulb. 5,000 for a new sink. 82bn total repayment for 13bn capital.

contracts.department: Department for Education
contracts.connection: Policy-driven PFI mandate. Balfour Beatty, Innisfree, and others extracted 140m+ in dividends from Yorkshire/Humber alone.
contracts.outcome: Total lifetime cost 30bn for 7.5bn capital. Schools paying 470k/year in PFI fees. NDAs on contract terms. 40% more expensive than public financing.

contracts.department: Cabinet Office
contracts.connection: Run by Rachel Wolf and James Frayne, both former advisers to Michael Gove. Awarded without competition.
contracts.outcome: High Court ruled unlawful (June 2021). Apparent bias in award. NAO flagged missing documentation.

contracts.department: Cabinet Office
contracts.product: 3 data analytics contracts for COVID response
contracts.connection: Faculty linked to Vote Leave and Dominic Cummings. 2 of 3 contracts had no conflict of interest documentation.
contracts.outcome: NAO flagged missing supplier selection justification and conflict of interest records.

contracts.department: Dept of Health and Social Care
contracts.product: Unused PPE, COVID tests, and vaccines written off
contracts.connection: Aggregate write-off of pandemic procurement. Includes 1bn of VIP lane PPE deemed unfit.
contracts.outcome: 15bn written off. Government clawed back only 3.4% of 1bn wasted VIP lane PPE.

sources: National Audit Office - Government procurement during COVID-19
Transparency International UK - Behind the Masks (2024)
Good Law Project - VIP Lane investigations
Best for Britain - Scandalous Spending Tracker
High Court judgments (PPE Medpro, VIP Lane, Public First)
House of Commons Public Accounts Committee
